# React Hooks: useState & useEffect (2026-06-12)

## useState Basics

```jsx
const [count, setCount] = useState(0);
const [user, setUser] = useState({ name: '', email: '' });
```

### State is Immutable

Always replace, don't mutate:

```jsx
// WRONG
user.name = 'Alice';
setUser(user);

// RIGHT
setUser({ ...user, name: 'Alice' });
// or with functional update
setUser((prev) => ({ ...prev, name: 'Alice' }));
```

## useEffect Patterns

### Mount only (empty deps)

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  fetchData();
}, []);
```

### Watch a dependency

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  document.title = `Count: ${count}`;
}, [count]);
```

### Cleanup on unmount

```jsx
useEffect(() => {
  const timer = setInterval(tick, 1000);
  return () => clearInterval(timer);
}, []);
```

## Common Pitfall

React StrictMode in development double-invokes effects. This is intentional — it helps catch missing cleanups. In production, effects run once.
